title: Battle of Corellia (1 ABY)
content:

{{Conjecture|legends}}

|objective=Protect [[Corellia]] from destruction as [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] escapes<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />
|date=[[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]<ref name="Madine">{{JournalCite|6|Galaxywide NewsNets}} places the outbreak of the [[Candorian plague/Legends|Candorian plague]] on [[Dentaal]] on 36:2:12, which falls in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. {{FactFile|130|MAD 1-4|General Crix Madine}} tie together the events of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' and [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], and Madine's own recollections in [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] as follows: upon learning of the [[Dark Trooper Project]], Madine alerts the Rebel Alliance to the project's existence, thus starting the events of ''Dark Forces''. Shortly afterwards, Madine is ordered to release the Candorian plague on Dentaal, an event which is dated to 36:2:12 per the Adventure Journal. Madine vanishes into the wilderness shortly afterwards, spending a week in the jungles of Dentaal before escaping and fleeing the planet—only to be captured almost immediately by General [[Rom Mohc]] and imprisoned on [[Orinackra]]. Kyle Katarn then frees Madine from Orinackra, and Madine is then involved in the [[Battle of Corellia (1 ABY)|battle]] at [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that only a short amount of time passed between Madine telling the Alliance about the Dark Troopers and his defection. The length of time Madine spends on the run places his capture and imprisonment on Orinackra firmly in the month 36:3, or the first month of [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Dark Forces'' prior to the [[mission to Orinackra]] fall in 0 ABY, and the remainder of the game and the battle at Corellia both occur in 1 ABY.</ref>

{{Quote|I've received word an Imperial officer, Crix Madine, wants to defect. Apparently he has a lot of information on the Empire's military ground operations. I'm gonna contact him after my meeting with the city officials in the capitol tower. Keep your eyes open for any sign of trouble.|General Rieekan's briefing|<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />}}
This '''battle''' took place on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. It occurred when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] officer [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], who was interested in joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], requested a proper meeting with the Alliance general [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] to discuss the terms of his defection. Three members of the elite Alliance flight [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] manning [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]], commanded by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], accompanied Rieekan to [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]], capital of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], to patrol the city for any signs of Imperial presence while the meeting took place.

An Imperial strike team interrupted the meeting and attempted to kill both Rieekan and the Imperial turncoat by destroying the [[Technology Center]] and the [[Coronet Capitol Tower]] while they were still inside. The timely intervention of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], as well as the arrival of [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|evacuation shuttles]], allowed for the Alliance to extract both Rieekan and Madine just before the Capitol Tower collapsed.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|This contemptible excuse for an officer will no longer divulge any more information to that Rebel, Kyle Katarn.|General Rom Mohc holding Madine at gunpoint|audio=ContemptibleExcuseForAnOfficer-DF.flac|<ref name="dark forces" />}}
After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] lost the [[Search for the Nonnah|search]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Nonnah]]'', internal security in the Empire was tightened, and any hint of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] was dealt with swiftly.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> Imperial [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] had attempted to defect to the Alliance prior to the Battle of Yavin, and, to prove his loyalty, Madine provided essential guidance for the sabotage of [[Operation Strike Fear]] and the destruction of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']]. However, it was the order to release the [[Candorian plague/Legends|Candorian plague]] on [[Dentaal]] that convinced Madine it was time to leave for good.<ref name="darksaber">[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]</ref>

Wracked with guilt over his role in the death of the entire Dentaalian population, Madine left his unit while performing a training mission in [[1 ABY/Legends|1.8 ABY]]. Madine erased himself from Imperial records, although his disappearance was covered by some [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] news channels. He did not even tell his fiancée where he was going, not wanting to place her in a dangerous situation.<ref name="darksaber" />

During this period, Madine provided clues to the Rebellion about the [[Dark Trooper Project]]; eventually he was captured by General [[Rom Mohc]] and imprisoned in [[Orinackra]], where he awaited execution. Upon learning of his capture, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] dispatched [[Kyle Katarn]] to [[Mission to Orinackra|rescue]] Madine, who revealed Mohc's role in the project. He also provided the Alliance with intelligence on two subsequent targets to sabotage the Dark Trooper project.<ref name="dark forces" />

Following his escape from Orinackra, Madine fled to his [[Unidentified Rebel safehouse (Coronet City)|safehouse]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. From there, he requested a meeting with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] of the Rebel Alliance to discuss his defection. Rieekan accepted his request and was escorted to [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]] by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], under the command of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Before meeting with Madine, Rieekan was to attend a conference with Coronet City officials in the [[Coronet Capitol Tower]].<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|That was never my favorite building anyway.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] watching TIE Bombers destroy the Capitol Tower|<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />}}
Commander [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and a detachment of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] were assigned to patrol the city in their [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] for any signs of Imperial activity. After [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] picked up strange transmissions, they flew to the outskirts of the city and found a squad of 5 [[Viper probe droid/Legends|probe droids]]. It turned out to be a diversion, however, in which squadrons of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] attacked the Capitol Tower during the Rogues' absence.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

[[File:Battle_of_Corellia_RS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Imperial forces assaulting Coronet.]]
Rogue Squadron rushed back to defend the city, while another squadron of 4 TIE bombers moved in on the Technology Center in which Madine was hiding. The Rogues had routed most of the bombers attacking the Tower before they received a transmission from Madine, stating that the Imperials had begun attacking the Center. Skywalker left to protect him, as the rest of Rogue Squadron, along with the new arrival of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], took care of the remaining bombers at the Capitol Tower.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

[[File:Battle_of_Corellia_RS_2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|TIE bombers attacking the Technology Center.]]
By the time the Technology Center was safe, the Imperials had deployed an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] walker, which moved to attack the Tower.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> It was then that Luke used Imperial defector [[Davin Felth/Legends|Davin Felth's]] advice,<ref name="Stormtrooper tale">{{StoryCite|story=When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> and tripped up the walker with his tow cable. After the remaining bombers were destroyed, the ''Falcon'' and a Rebel [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] began evacuating the Capitol Tower. At the same time, stormtroopers invaded the beaches in [[Amphibion]]s, but the Rebel [[DF.9/Legends|defense batteries]] managed to keep most of the troops out of the city.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

The Imperials resumed their attack on Madine with a pair of [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers leading another AT-AT, while a squad of Stormtroopers attempted to break into the Technology Center. Luke drove the Imperials off, while a second evacuation shuttle arrived to pick up Madine. With Madine's successful defection, and the evacuation of the Capitol Tower, Rogue Squadron withdrew from the planet. The Capitol Tower was destroyed as soon as the Rebel force departed.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" />

==Aftermath==
Madine would go on to be a successful Rebel Alliance general, serving in battles such as the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="jedi">{{Film|VI}}</ref> and would go on to be a general of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. One of his first missions with the Alliance was commanding [[Gold Squadron]] during the [[Liberation of Gerrard V]], during which he would once again be involved with the actions of Rogue Squadron.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], while leading a commando team to sabotage the [[Darksaber (superweapon)|Darksaber]] superweapon, Madine was captured and killed by [[Durga Besadii Tai]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]].<ref name="darksaber" /> In his honor, the [[General Crix Madine Military Reserve]] was constructed over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the reformation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]].<ref name="tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Rogue Squadron's mission to rescue General Rieekan and Crix Madine first appeared in the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], as the game's fourth mission, "Defection at Corellia".<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> In this level, the player for the first time is required to use the harpoon and tow cable of a [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]] against an AT-AT in order to complete the mission successfully. As the Battle of Corellia takes place in the timeline prior to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], during which [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] supposedly devised the strategy for downing the four-legged walkers, this element of the mission ''may'' be non-canon and chalked up to game mechanics.

Han Solo's entrance into the battle, in which he saves a Rebel pilot by swooping down from above, and then shooting down the TIE bombers chasing him, all while shouting "Yee-haw!", is similar to how he saves Luke Skywalker at the Battle of Yavin.

If the Nintendo 64 version of ''Rogue Squadron'' is played with the default Jumper Pak installed in the console, all TIE fighters that are present in this level are replaced with TIE bombers due to technical limitations in the game's memory.
